properties.remark: REPORT OF 5 INCHES OF WATER RUNNING DOWN THE STREET IN NORTH SAN BERNARDINO NEIGHBORHOOD.

FIREFIGTHERS RESPONDED TO CALL OF 70 FOOT PINE TREE ON HOME AND VEHICLE. POWER LINES KNOCKED OUT. LEADING LINE OF HEAVY RAIN AT THE TIME. SEVERAL LIGHTNING STRIKES WESTERN SAN BERNADINO MOUNTAINS 10 AM TO 11 AM ON SAME LINE OF STORMS. UPDATE TO REPORT FOR MANZANITA AVE WIND DAMAGE TO CORRECT TIME. THE TREE BLOCKED THE STREET.
FLASH FLOODING OVER ARABY DRIVE NEAR THE WASH IN PALM SPRINGS.
HEAVY DEBRIS FLOW ALONG HIGHWAY 18 NEAR THE ARROWBEAR AREA. ARROWBEAR MUSIC CAMP WILL BE BLOCKED OFF.
RIVERSIDE OES REPORTS ORTEGA HIGHWAY CLOSED DUE TO DEBRIS FLOW OF MUD AROUND FALLS FIRE SCAR. SINK HOLE REPORTED ON GRAND AVENUE DEBRIS FLOW DURATION 2 HOURS
DUST/SAND STORM IN BORREGO SPRINGS. VISIBILITY REDUCED TO A QUARTER MILE WITH WINDS GUSTING TO 45 MPH.

JOHN WAYNE AIRPORT OFFICIALS REPORTED A PLANE MOVED 3 FEET FROM SEVERE WIND, SEVERAL METAL SIGNS DOWN AND JET BRIDGE DAMAGE. ASOS HAD 35 MPH. LIKELY WET MICROBURST AS SEEN ON RADAR LINE SEGMENT. DOWNSTREAM WIND MEASURED AT RAWS SITE AT 74 MPH.
TREE LANDED ON ROOF OF HOME FROM STRONG WINDS IN LINE OF HEAVY RAIN. EMBEDDED THUNDER REPORTED WITH THIS LINE.
MESONET STATION FREMONT CANYON /FRCC1/ LINE OF HEAVY RAIN WET MICROSBURST. PEAK WIND IS PAST HOUR.
MESONET STATION CORONADO CAYS /XJST/ WEATHERFLOW MESONET. RADAR SHOWED INTENSE LINE SEGMENT OVERHEAD TO SUPPORT WET MICROBURST OF STRONGER WINDS ALOFT.
ABC NEWS 10 REPORTS STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 60 MPH WHICH BROUGHT DOWN A LARGE TREE ONTO A CONDOMINIUM IN CHULA VISTA.
6 INCH TREE REPORTED DOWN, WIND GUSTS 45 MPH AT THE TIME ALONG LINE OF HEAVY SHOWERS
UPDATE TO PRIOR REPORT. NBC MEDIA SHOWED VIDEO SHOWED 6 TO 12 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER OVER COUNTRY CLUB DRIVE. TRUCK DRIVING THROUGH CLOSED ROAD HARMONY GROVE.
FOLLOW UP TO THE LEVEE FAILURE ALONG SHEEP CREEK IN PINON HILLS. EIGHT FAMILIES WERE EVACUATED FROM THE AREA...AS THEIR HOMES WERE THREATENED BY THE FLOOD WATERS.

PUBLIC WORKS IS REPORTING 1200 CUBIC TONS OF DEBRIS ACROSS THE ROADS IN FOREST FALLS. THE ROAD IS CLOSED...SINCE THEY ARE STILL CLEARING THE DEBRIS FROM YESTERDAY. HOWEVER...THE RAIN HAS TURNED TO SNOW.
WATER SPOUT 3/4 MILE OFF SHORE OF CARLSBAD OBSERVED BY SKYWARN OBSERVERS. DISTINCTIVE SWIRL OF WATER AT OCEAN SURFACE.
WATERSPOUT CYCLED TWICE AND LASTED ABOUT 5 MINUTES

MULTIPLE CARS CRASHED ON HWY 86 NEAR THERMAL/MECCA DUE TO ZERO VISIBLITY FROM HIGH WINDS BLOWING DUST ACCORDING TO CHP. CHP ISSUED SIG ALERT FOLLOWING THE CRASH...ALL SOUTHBOUND LANES ON HWY 86 WERE CLOSED. KTRM AIRPORT AT TIME REPORTING WINDS WSW 22 GUSTS TO 37 MPH. COACHELLA WEATHER OBSERVATION WINDS NW 15 MPH GUSTING TO 31 MPH.

GENE AUTRY TRAIL AND VISTA CHINO ARE BOTH CLOSE AT THE WASH DUE TO LOW VISIBILTY FROM BLOWING DUST. POLICE MADE THE DECISION JUST BEFORE 230 PM. WINDS AT THE TIME...EDOM HILL NW 50 MPH WITH GUSTS TO 68 MPH...KPSP AIRPORT NE 25 MPH WITH GUSTS 38 MPH.
AIRLINE PILOT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 3 MILES SOUTHWEST OF LINDBERGH FIELD...NEAR NORTH ISLAND NAVAL AIR STATION.
PALM SPRINGS PD HAS INDIAN CANYON AND GENE AUTRY JSO 10 CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WINDS AND SAND.
PALM SPRINGS PD CLOSED VISTA CHINO DUE TO HIGH WINDS AND SAND.
HIGHWAY PATROL IS REPORTING A FLASH FLOOD ALONG SR78 AT YAQUI PASS. SEVERAL BOULDERS ARE IN THE ROAD WAY, AND A SIGALERT HAS BEEN ISSUED.

CHP REPORTED A MUDSLIDE OVER THE BORREGO SPRINGS ROAD AT SAN PABLO ROAD, WITH EQUIPMENT NEEDED TO SCRAP THE ROADWAY. ROCKS, SAND, AND STANDING WATER ALSO EXTENDING TO NEAR TILTING T DRIVE AND CHRISTMAS CIRCLE.
TWO STRUCTURES DAMAGED. ONE ROOF COLAPSED AND ONE ROOF WITH WIND DAMAGE BETWEEN 130 PM PDT AND 2 PM PDT NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CALIMESA AND INTERSTATE 10.
TRANSIENT RESCUED FROM FLOWING WATER IN FLOOD CONTROL CHANNEL AT 11TH AND D STREET IN YUCAIPA.

FLASH FLOODING AT HIGHWAY 66 (NATIONAL TRAILS HWY) AND HODGE RD.
CHP HAS REPORTED A WASH OUT FROM HEAVY RAINFALL AT SR-247 AND RECHE ROAD...AS WELL AS DEBRIS ON THE ROADWAY. CHP HAS DECIDED TO CLOSE SR-247 AT THIS INTERSECTION.

CHP REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ON HIGHWAY 79 AT FINK RD. CHP CLOSED THE HIGHWAY AT MILE MARKER 46.
CHP REPORTED A ROCK SLIDE ON SUNRISE HIGHWAY PARTIALLY BLOCKING NORTHBOUND LANE.

CHP REPORTED WATER OVER THE HIGHWAY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 79 AND CHIHUAHUA VALLEY RD WITH TRAFFIC STOPPED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS. SPOTTER ALSO REPORTED THE ROADWAY WASHED OUT AT THE SAME LOCATION.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS ROADWAY FLOODING ON DANBURY STREET IN BANNING...WITH THE ROADWAY IMPASSABLE.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS ROADWAY FLOODING ON DANBURY STREET IN CABAZON. FLOOD WATER...ROCKS MUD AND DEBRIS FLOWING DOWN THE STREET...AND THE ROADWAY IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO BOULDERS AND MUD BLOCKING THE STREET.

CAL OES SAN BERNARDINO REPORTED FLASH FLOODING AND DEBRIS FLOW IN MILL CREEK. PEOPLE BEING EVACUATED FROM THE CREEK BASIN.
ROCK SLIDE REPORTED AT INTERSECTION OF YAQUI PASS ROAD AND BORREGO SPRINGS ROAD...WITH CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL SAYING THAT THE ROAD NEEDS TO BE SHUT DOWN AND PLOWED.

FLASH FLOOD IN PROGRESS IN FOREST FALLS AREA. COUNTY FIRE SHUTTING DOWN VALLEY OF THE FALLS DRIVE AT HIGHWAY 38. SHELTER IN PLACE PROTOCOLS WERE GIVEN BY COUNTY FIRE DEPARTMENT. MULTIPLE VEHICLES REPORTED IN FLOODED WASH AND PROPANE TANKS WERE DISLODGED. SWIFT WATER RESCUES IN PROGRESS. AREA CLOSED TO NON-RESIDENTS.

SPOTTER REPORTS FLOODING OF STREETS...DRY LAKES FLOODED AND DRY LAKE AREAS FLOODED.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS ROCK SLIDE ON MOUNT BALDY ROAD AND HAS REQUESTED THAT THE ROAD BE CLOSED. THIS APPEARS TO HAVE OCCURRED JUST EAST OF THE LA COUNTY LINE.
DAMAGE FROM FLOODING AND A PROPANE TANK RUPTURE AT THE MOUNT BALDY VISITOR CENTER. MANY ROADS WASHED OUT IN THE AREA. DIRECT CAUSE AND EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE UNKNOWN

USGS GAGE IN THE PERRIS VALLEY STORM DRAIN AT NUEVO RD SHOWED A SPIKE FROM 8.68 FT TO 14.94 FT. BY 10 PM. THE INITIAL RISE OF 3 FT. OCCURRED IN LESS THANK 30 MINUTES. FLASH FLOOD DURATION 2 HOURS 15 MINUTES.

CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FORESTRY REPORTS SIGNIFICANT FLOODING IN THE CITY OF HIGHLAND IN THE AREA OF BASELINE ROAD. EVERYONE IS ADVISED TO SHELTER IN PLACE AND THEY ARE CHECKING FOR FOLKS STUCK IN WATER/MUD.

DEBRIS FLOW OBSERVED WITH 18 TO 24 INCH MUD BEING REMOVED BY CALTRANS. CULVERT HAD OVERFLOWED ONTO ROAD. THIS IS DIRECTLY BELOW A RECENT WILDFIRE SCARE ON THE BANNER GRADE. TIME IS APPROXIMATE. CLEAN UP WAS ONGOING ON AUGUST 13 AT 830 AM.

MUD AND WATER FLOWED ACROSS GENE AUTRY TRAIL AND VISTA CHINO ROADS WHERE THEY INTERSECT BEGINNING AT 230 AM. ROADS WERE CLOSED UNTIL ABOUT 2 PM

*** 1 INJ *** HABOOB IN PALM SPRINGS FELLED A TREE ONTO A PICKUP TRUCK CONTAINING A MAN WHO WAS INJURED AND TAKEN TO HOSPITAL. PEAK WIND SPEEDS AT NEARBY PALM SPRINGS WERE 40 MPH AT THAT TIME.
SPOTTER REPORTS A DUST STORM PRODUCING QUARTER MILE VISIBILITY IN CATHEDRAL CITY...WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 15-20 MPH.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS ROAD WASHING OUT AT CEDAR AVENUE IN HEMET.

OFF-ROAD VEHICLE STUCK IN FLASH FLOOD, AS WELL AS ANOTHER PARTY OF 2STUCK IN A HOUSE, CAN'T CROSS ROAD DUE TO WATER. COUNTY ROADS CLOSED HIGHWAY 74 AT THE CROSS STREET 1.5 MILES FROM THE RANGER STATION, DOE CANYON ROAD.
FLOODING ALONG HIGHWAY 74 AT 1ST STREET ACROSS ALL LANES OF 74. ALSO FLOODING AT PALM AND 1ST, WITH KNEE-DEEP WATER.
